name: Aplete
identity: A creative universe from Las Vegas — songs, art and ideas from multi-instrumentalist Stefano Kajatt.

name: AURORA
identity: A voice from the Norwegian fjords, singing for nature, warriors, and weirdos alike.

name: Beats Antique
identity: A traveling mechanical circus of Turkish banjo, marching drums, and glitching bass.

name: Béla Fleck and the Flecktones
identity: Banjo-led fusioneers folding bluegrass, jazz, funk, and the future into one band.

name: Björk
identity: Icelandic singer, composer and world-builder, four decades into remaking pop in her own image.

name: Bonobo
identity: Simon Green, the Brighton-born producer who turned downtempo electronica into a full live orchestra of sound.

name: Chance the Rapper
identity: Chicago's independent son, who gave his music away free and won Grammys doing it.

name: Esperanza Spalding
identity: Bassist, vocalist and composer bending jazz toward chamber music, art-rock and ritual song.

name: Florence + the Machine
identity: Cathedral-sized art rock built on Florence Welch's hurricane of a voice.

name: Gorillaz
identity: Damon Albarn and Jamie Hewlett's animated band — pop's longest-running cartoon experiment.

name: Hiatus Kaiyote
identity: Future-soul four-piece bending jazz, funk and electronics into something wholly Melbourne.

name: Hundred Waters
identity: Fragile electronics and Nicole Miglis' hushed voice, ringing like a bell from somewhere far off.

name: Imogen Heap
identity: Self-produced electronic pop from a songwriter who builds her own instruments.

name: James Blake
identity: A choirboy's voice suspended in sub-bass — songs built from silence as much as sound.

name: Jesca Hoop
identity: A Californian folk experimentalist weaving strange, luminous songs from her adopted home in Manchester.

name: Joanna Newsom
identity: Harpist, pianist, and singer of long, luminous story-songs from the Sierra foothills.

name: MF DOOM
identity: The man behind the metal mask — hip-hop's supervillain laureate, 1971–2020. ALL CAPS when you spell the man name.

name: Modest Mouse
identity: Jagged, restless Pacific Northwest indie rock asking big questions in a cracked voice.

name: Nick Drake
identity: A voice like evening light on an English garden — gone at 26, still arriving.

name: Oumou Sangaré
identity: The Songbird of Wassoulou, carrying Mali's hunter-society rhythms and women's stories to the world.

name: Radiohead
identity: Five musicians from Oxfordshire who keep reinventing what a rock band can be.

name: Sigur Rós
identity: Icelandic post-rock voyagers who turn glacial landscape into sound.

name: Tame Impala
identity: Kevin Parker's one-man psychedelic universe from Perth, built alone in the studio and unleashed as a band onstage.

name: The Beatles
identity: Four Liverpudlians who turned eight years of records into the common language of pop.

name: The Boswell Sisters
identity: Three sisters from uptown New Orleans who bent tempo, key, and radio itself to their harmonies.

name: The Smile
identity: Thom Yorke, Jonny Greenwood, and Tom Skinner — Radiohead's restless core rewired through jazz drumming and krautrock nerve.

name: Thom Yorke
identity: A falsetto threaded through circuitry — Radiohead's voice, wandering alone into the glitch.

name: Tool
identity: Los Angeles art-metal architects turning odd time signatures into ritual.

name: Trust in the Sun
identity: A buttery Las Vegas fusion of jazz, soul, funk and pop.
